# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

- About Us
- Research and Facilities
- Ph.D Students

The Department of Civil Engineering (CE) of IIT Jammu, one of six engineering departments, was started in the year 2017. The department offers four-year undergraduate courses, training the students in multiple core disciplines including Structural Analysis and Design, Traffic Engineering, Hydraulics, Construction Management, Environment Engineering, and Surveying, as well as exposure to interdisciplinary projects to enhance collaborative efforts and hone entrepreneurship skills. The department aligns its vision of being the collaborative innovation ambassador in Civil Engineering with the Institute’s mission objectives of passion for attempting to solve problems innovatively with a culture of mutual respect and creativity. Our mission is to offer world-class undergraduate and graduate education, research outcomes, industrial consultancy, and training in Civil Engineering.

Civil Engineering, globally, is at the verge of disruptive technology for sustainable construction methods, materials and energy efficient infrastructure. In the ever-evolving industrial scenario, the contents delivered to the students are regularly updated to successfully cope with these changes. The department also has research programs supported through government and industry funding. The growing department has a faculty strength of 11, with persons active in research fields of Bridge Engineering, Seismic Analysis, Ultra High Performance Concrete, Nondestructive Evaluation and Structural Health Monitoring, Fracture Mechanics, Novel Engineering Materials, Fatigue Modeling, Modeling of Fractured Materials, Computational Mechanics, Public Transportation, Rapid Transit Systems, Earthquake Risk Evaluation, Seismic retrofitting, Wind-structure Interaction, Groundwater and Contaminant Transport, Drought Modelling.

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

Civil EngineeringAnkit Kathuria

### **Ankit Kathuria**

Associate Professor

#### Brief Info Get to know the iMaSS lab, click the link for details: https://sites.google.com/view/imass **About me** Dr. Ankit Kathuria is working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering and Humanities and Social Sciences Department at IIT Jammu. Throughout his career, he has undertaken diverse roles, including his current position as Associate Dean for E-Governance System and his previous role as Head of the Department of Civil Engineering and Associate Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management). He received the prestigious SERB-SIRE Fellowship in 2022, allowing him to contribute to significant research related to roundabout safety at the Future Mobility Group, Newcastle University, UK, for six months. Dr. Kathuria did postdoctoral research at the Indian Institute of Technology in Bombay, India. He obtained his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2017. He received a Masters in Planning degree with specialization in Transport Planning from the School of Planning and Architecture, New Delhi in 2013 and, holds a Bachelor of Technology degree in Civil Engineering from Jaypee University, India. In the realm of research, Dr. Kathuria has led various projects funded by esteemed organizations such as the Ministry of Road Transportation and Highways, Science and Engineering Research Board, National Highway Authority of India, Central Road Research Institute, Jammu Kashmir Science Technology and Innovation Council, and Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. Dr. Kathuria's focus on research is driven by a genuine desire to find practical solutions to various transportation planning and engineering problems. His research encompasses a range of disciplines, including behavioral and psychological aspects of traffic, road safety, public transportation system design and planning, traffic management using BT and wifi sensors, social sciences, data mining, mathematical modeling, and flexible pavements (secondary research area). Presently, he leads six research projects as the principal investigator, aligning with his research interests. In his mentoring capacity, Dr. Kathuria guides seven Ph.D. students, out of which one student has already submitted his thesis for further evaluation. He is grateful for the opportunities to contribute to academic discourse, with over 47 peer-reviewed research and conference papers to his name. Additionally, he is honored to serve as a reviewer for respected journals such as TR part A, TR part D, Accident Analysis and Prevention, Case Study on Transport Policy, Transport Policy, Transportation Letters and Journal of Transportation Engineering. Dr. Kathuria remains deeply committed to advancing knowledge and expertise in
the humble pursuit of contributing to the field of transportation Planning and engineering.

#### Personal Website

https://sites.google.com/view/imass

#### Research Interest

**Primary research interest**

a) Behavioural and psychological aspects of drivers in heterogeneous disordered traffic conditions

b) Proactive approaches to road safety, including the use of surrogate safety measures

c) Planning and design of dedicated powered two-wheeler lanes for urban and non-urban highways

d) Simulation-based AV's and human-driven vehicle interaction

e) Public transportation system design and planning with primary focus on Bus Rapid Transit Systems

f) Exploring adoption intentions of e-shared mobility alternatives

g) Traffic management using BT and Wifi sensors

**Secondary research interest**

h) Utilization of surface-modified aggregates in asphalt mixes

#### Academic Interests

I have experience teaching both undergraduate and postgraduate students at IIT Jammu. The courses I have taught or am currently teaching are as follows:

1. Transportation and Highway Engineering
2. Traffic Safety and Environment
3. Traffic Flow Modelling
4. Contracts and Legal Aspects in Tunnel Engineering
5. Introduction to Transportation Engineering
6. Computer-Aided Design (Product Visualization)
7. Public Transportation
8. Road Safety

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

Civil EngineeringAnurag Misra

### **Anurag Misra**

Professor

#### Brief Info

I am working as Professor in Civil Engineering Department and Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management). Before joining IIT Jammu, I worked as Chief Executive Officer of an infrastructure company where I was taking care of planning, designing and execution of Pre-engineered steel warehousing complexes and steel-concrete composite elevated road project. I also played a pivotal role in expanding the company and establishment of an innovative automated manufacturing unit, which is a state-of-the-art facility for manufacturing precast sections and heavy prefab steel structures for public infrastructure sector & prefab multi storey buildings.

Before taking up this industrial assignment, I worked in academia as Professor & Head of Structural Engineering, Coordinator of Centre for Building Technology and Construction Materials at Malaviya National Institute of Technology, Jaipur and Director of Anand International College of Engineering, Jaipur. I also worked at Liverpool John Moores University, Liverpool (U.K.) and Curtins Consulting Engineers (U.K.) under U.K. India REC’s Project for six months in the area of Structural Design and Construction.

My research focuses on Utilization of Wastes in Concrete, Durability of Concrete, Concrete Repairs and Non-destructive Testing of Concrete. I have been involved in supervising projects in the area of Durability of High-Volume Fly ash Concrete, Self-compacting Concrete, Use of Wollastonite in Concrete and Durability of Concrete Containing ISF Slag, Geopolymer Concrete, Use of Jarosite in Concrete, etc. I have also been associated with collaborative research project "High Performance Sustainable Concrete Infrastructure" jointly with University of Dundee (U.K.) and eight Indian Institutions, funded by UK India Education and research Initiative (UKIERI). I also executed more than 200 consultancy projects including Jaipur Metro Project.

Presently I am looking for motivated Post-Doctoral/ PhD/ Internship candidates who would be interested in working on development of low carbon cement, Non-destructive Testing with IoT applications and Prestressed Composite bridges, High Strength Steel welding. Interested persons may send an email: anurag.misra@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Education Qualification

- B.E. (Civil), 1987 University of Rajasthan (MREC Jaipur now MNIT Jaipur)
- M.Tech (Structures), 1993, Kurukshetra University (REC Kurukshetra now NIT Kurukshetra)
- Ph.D. 2004, The Queen's University of Belfast (U.K.)

#### Academic Interests

**Research Interests**

Utilisation of wastes in concrete, improvements in cover-zone properties of concrete, Performance of High strength steel-concrete composite sections. Polymer composites, High strength steel welding

**Teaching Interests**

Strength of Materials and Theory of Structures, Design of Concrete Structures, Design of Steel structures, Pre-stressed Structures, Bridge Engineering

### **Deepak Yadav**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Deepak Yadav is presently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu. He did PhD in Structural Engineering from IIT Delhi and BTech in Civil Engineering from IIT Roorkee. His doctoral research was focused on metallic-yielding dampers and he filed a patent on the developed damper as co-inventor. His research interests are seismic response control, retrofitting of structures, steel and aluminum structures, and earthquake-resistant design. Dr. Yadav also has industrial experience in the structural design of buildings in Africa, Middle East, and India regions. He has been a part of more than twenty projects as a structural engineer and peer reviewer.

Civil EngineeringDivyesh Varade

### **Divyesh Varade**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Divyesh Varade is a budding expert on remote sensing and spatial analysis. Dr. Varade is working as the Assistant Professor of Geomatics Engineering at the Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, since 2020. He received his PhD degree in Civil Engineering with specialization in Geoinformatics from IIT Kanpur in 2020 and his master’s degree in the International Program on Earth Oriented Space Science and Technology at the Technical University of Munich. He received the IEEE Senior Member title in November 2023, received the runner-up award for best PhD thesis by IEEE GRSS India in 2020. He has served several reputed journals as a referee with more than 100 listed reviews, and is also serving as the review editor for Frontiers in Remote Sensing for the microwave remote sensing section. He is also serving as the editorial board member for the journal All Earth. His primary research interests are focused on deepening the understanding of the cryosphere using remote sensing, agricultural applications of microwave remote sensing, and inversion of surface geophysical parameters. His expertise on remote sensing (multispectral, hyperspectral, thermal, microwave) integrates physical and mathematical modelling, information theory, statistical analysis and machine learning. His research interests further include studies on glaciers, sea ice, and permafrost. His research also includes deriving remote sensing-based solutions to broader problems in Earth observation, agriculture, and water resources.

#### Professional Background

Dr. Divyesh M. Varade is an expert on remote sensing encompassing the domains of hyperspectral, thermal, and microwave remote sensing. Dr. Varade is presently working as the Assistant Professor of Geomatics at the Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. He is a research collaborator at the ATAL Centre for Artificial Intelligence at MJPRU-Bareilly and an advisor/consultant to Geo Climate Risk Solutions Pvt. Ltd. He received his Ph.D. degree in Geoinformatics from the Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Kanpur in 2020. He was the IEEE GRSS India runner-up awardee for the best Ph.D. thesis category in 2020. In his doctoral research, he devised novel methods for the qualitative and quantitative characterization of snow using multi-sensor remote sensing data. His research integrates physical and mathematical modelling, information theory, statistical analysis, and machine learning. He is also avidly interested in the hydrological and environmental problems related to the high mountain regions and polar sea ice. Prior to his Ph.D. Dr. Varade pursued his graduate degree in the International Earth Oriented Space Science and Technology (ESPACE) Program with a specialization in remote sensing at the Technical University of Munich. He conducted his graduate research on change of detection of buildings at the Division of Photogrammetry and Image Analysis, Remote Sensing Technology Institute, German Aerospace Centre. Dr. Varade holds an undergraduate degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering, which significantly influenced his interests in radar remote sensing.

Dr. Varade has published well-received articles on snow geophysics and snowpack characterization. He is a referee for several highly reputed hydrology/water resource and remote sensing journals. He is also a member of several international associations on remote sensing, hydrology/water resources, and cryosphere. His research interests are developing novel methods using remote sensing for problems broadly related to the cryosphere, hydrology, water resources, and environmental issues. Dr. Varade is also avidly interested in applying AI/ML techniques for qualitative and quantitative problems in Earth observation. He has reviewed several manuscripts on applied ML with remote sensing in sea ice and snow studies and is presently working on the application of deep learning techniques for the identification of avalanche debris, snow depth mapping, and various other problems in Cryosphere.

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

Civil EngineeringPrasun Halder

### **Prasun Halder**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Prasun Halder has been serving the Civil Engineering Department of IIT Jammu as an Assistant Professor since March 2022. Before joining IIT Jammu, he was a Research Fellow at IIT Delhi. Dr. Halder holds a Ph.D. in Geotechnical Engineering from IIT Delhi.

His basic research interest lies in experimental and computational geomechanics with focus on foundation engineering, dam-foundation system on jointed rock, ground improvement, and soil dynamics. Currently, his research group at IIT Jammu is working on applied geomechanics domain to better understand the transportation geotechnics in hilly regions, application of geosynthetic-based solution for rock-fall hazard, tunnel & underground space, and cold-weather geotechnics.

Dr. Halder publishes extensively in journals and conferences of international repute and has successfully secured multiple research projects from funding agencies such as SERB (presently ANRF), Ministry of Textiles (NTTM), and MoRTH. He has received several awards and recognitions from reputed research agencies. He is involved in various professional societies, including the Indian Geotechnical Society and the International Society of Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering. ​

#### Book/Book Chapter ***BOOK:*** "Modular Treatment Approach for Drinking Water and Wastewater" (Eds. Satinder Kaur Brar, Pratik Kumar, Agnieszka Cuprys). Elsevier Publications, ISBN: 0323854222, 9780323854221 **About the Book:** This book titled "Modular Treatment Approach for Drinking Water and Wastewater" explores the most up-to-date studies in the field of treating water. Through three contributed sections, this comprehensive resource presents the new approach of tackling the demand for a high quality, economic and green water treatment system. This modular strategy allows for a customized retrofit solution to constantly changing parameters that are dependent on current demand and requirements. The book summarizes the principles of modular design, as well as current developments and perspectives. Chapters include global case studies, flow diagrams and charts to provide readers with the tools they need to implement these techniques in their own work. As such, this book is a must have for researchers, students and professors working in water and wastewater. ***BOOK CHAPTERS:*** 1. ### **Riya Bhowmik**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Riya Bhowmik is an Assistant Professor of Civil Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. ​ She holds a Ph.D. in Geotechnical and Geoenvironmental Engineering from IIT Delhi. Dr. Bhowmik has been with IIT Jammu since October 2020 and has also served as a Visiting Fellow at the University of Salerno, Italy.

Her research focuses on geosynthetics, ground improvement, and disaster mitigation. ​ Dr. Bhowmik publishes extensively in international journals and conferences, and has successfully secured several research projects from funding agencies such as SERB (presently ANRF) and Ministry of Textiles. ​She has been recognized as one of the 75 Influential Indian Women in Geotechnical Engineering and has received several awards, including the SERB National Post-Doctoral Fellowship and the Indian Geotechnical Society-M.D. Desai Memorial YGE Award. ​ Dr. Bhowmik is involved in various professional societies, including the Indian Geotechnical Society and the International Society of Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering. ​

#### Current Openings

***Research Positions for prospective Ph.D Students:*** Available

Motivated, bright, and sincere candidates with strong academic background in **Civil and Geotechnical Engineering**, and preferable expertise in **Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering, Geosynthetics, and Numerical and Experimental Geotechnics**may email their CV (preferably with list of publications) with a cover letter (1 page). The candidates are also requested to visit the Homepage of IIT Jammu for regular updates on admissions to various programs.

#### Research Interest

**Research interests**

- Protection works against landslides (debris flow/rockfall/mudslides) in hilly regions
- Use of geosynthetics for hill road infrastructure
- Rock-shed tunnels under rockfall impacts
- Geosynthetics-reinforced infrastructure in seasonally frozen regions
- Tunnelling-induced instability and surface settlements

### **Ved Prakash Ranjan**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Ved Prakash Ranjan is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu, specializing in environmental sustainability, circular economy, and waste management. Previously, he was a postdoctoral researcher in the Circular Chemical Engineering division of the Faculty of Science and Engineering at Maastricht University, The Netherlands.

His research focuses on **enhancing the recyclability of end-of-life vehicle (ELV) plastics**, optimizing **pre-treatment processes**, and improving **feedstock quality** to drive **sustainable material recovery**. He is particularly interested in addressing the **identification challenges of black plastics**, a growing concern in **automotive and waste electrical and electronic equipment** (**WEEE) plastic recycling industries**, and assessing the **performance indicators of compounded materials**.

His earlier work extensively explored the **formation, distribution, and impact of microplastics (MPs)** in technical and environmental systems. He is dedicated to developing **efficient methodologies for detecting, identifying, and quantifying MPs**, aiming to systematically understand their **fate, transport, and interactions with organic and chemical pollutants**.

Through his research and academic engagement, he strives to contribute to **circular economy solutions, sustainable waste management strategies, and innovative recycling approaches** that bridge **scientific advancements with industry applications**.

**Keywords:** ELV Plastics; Resource recovery; Circular economy; Solid waste management; Plastic waste management; Recyclability assessment; Fate and Transport of Microplastic; Standardization of Microplastic Assessment

#### Research Interest

Solid Waste Management and other areas in environmental engineering

#### Education Qualification

PhD (IIT Kharagpur, PASSING YEAR 2022); Post doc (Maastricht University, July 2024-Dec 2024); 6 month

#### Academic Interests

Teaching, research and consultancy

Divyesh Varade Full Name Divyesh Manohar Varade Date of Birth 20/02/1987 Address Office 11AC-5039, Pushkar Building, Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, Jagti, NH-44, PO Nagrota, Jammu - 181 221 J&K, India Contact E-mail: divyesh.varade@iitjammu.ac.in, dmvarade@ieee.org Phone(O): +91-191-274-1171 Research Profiles researchgate.net/profile/Divyesh_Varade  orcid.org/0000-0003-0283-8387  scholar.google.com/citations?user=qyPvPvsAAAAJ&hl=en  Citations: 685, h-index: 1, i-index: 23  Website: https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/faculty/~divyeshvarade Bio-sketch Dr. Divyesh Varade is a budding expert on remote sensing and spatial analysis. Dr. Varade is working as the Assistant Professor of Geomatics Engineering at the Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, since 2020. He received his PhD rnational Program on Earth Oriented Space Science and Technology at the Technical University of Munich. He received the IEEE Senior Member title in November 2023, received the runner-up award for best PhD thesis by IEEE GRSS India in 2020. He has served several reputed journals as a referee with more than 100 listed reviews, and is also serving as the review editor for Frontiers in Remote Sensing for the microwave remote sensing section. He is also serving as the editorial board member for the journal All Earth. His primary research interests are focused on deepening the understanding of the cryosphere using remote sensing, agricultural applications of microwave remote sensing, and inversion of surface geophysical parameters. His expertise on remote sensing (multispectral, hyperspectral, thermal, microwave) integrates physical and mathematical modelling, information theory, statistical analysis and machine learning. His research interests further include studies on glaciers, sea ice, and permafrost. His research also includes deriving remote sensing-based solutions to broader problems in Earth observation, agriculture, and water resources. # Page Title: Geotechnical Engineering

❮

❯

## Vision

Fostering disaster-resilient and sustainable infrastructure through specialized research in Himalayan terrains
and complex geo-materials.

Explore

## News and Updates

## Overview

The Geotechnical Engineering section at IIT Jammu bridges fundamental geomechanics with practical
engineering.
Strategically located in the Himalayan foothills, our program is uniquely positioned to address the complex
geotechnical challenges.

We specialize in the resilient design and performance of critical hill infrastructure, exploring the behavior
of
soil, rock, and geosynthetics to develop sustainable, disaster-resilient solutions for diverse geological and
climatic terrains.

## Why Geotechnical Engineering at IIT Jammu?

A living laboratory for Himalayan geotechnics

Merging GIS, Remote Sensing, and Machine Learning Approaches with core geotechnical engineering.

Focused on solving real-world infrastructure challenges in sensitive environments.

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

### Dr. Riya Bhowmik

#### HoD Civil Engineering

### Message

An affectionate welcome to the Civil Engineering discipline.Civil Engineering is a professional engineering discipline that deals with the design, construction and maintenance of physically built works like roads, bridges, canals, dams and buildings.

My vision is to develop constructive thinking and analytical capabilities of every student of CIVIL Department. Our department is committed to provide students with a strong, broad based fundamental engineering education and prepare the students for a career in the industry, teaching and national laboratories. We also plan to develop entrepreneurial skills in students so that they would drive the spirit of growth of our economy and would be able to generate employment opportunities for other qualified and skilled people. I look forward for preparing my students to face the challenges; the technology and engineering sector would offer in the future and succeed in offering technological solutions for the betterment of the society and our nation. Wishing all our students brilliant and bright future.

## Environmental Engineering Laboratory

**Atomic absorption spectrophotometer (AAS)**

**Make**: Shimadzu

**Model**: AA-6880

**Equipment Description**: Atomic Absorption Spectroscopy is an instrumental analysis technique for rapid trace metal analysis. HVG assembly is also attached with the instrument for the detection of metals like mercury, arsenic and selenium. Besides the specific characteristics of AA-6880 system are as AA-6880 Series supports a wide range of analysis applications such as environment, chemistry, food etc. 8-lamp turret. Easy to use user-friendly software. Advances in optics and a new graphite furnace design achieve improved lower limits of detection for furnace analysis. Trace Element Analysis by using Micro Sampling Technology.

**UV-VIS Spectrophotometer**

**Make**: Perkin Elmer

**Model**: UV/Vis Lambda365

**Equipment Description**:

1. Possible to measure in the wavelength of 190-1100 nm
2. When high stability and low stray light are critical, the LAMBDA 365 double-beam technology is the ideal solution

**Water quality analyser**

**Make**: Thermoscientific

**Model**: Orion Versastar Pro

**Equipment Description**: pH/ ISE/ Conductivity/ Dissolved Oxygen Multi-parameter Benchtop Meter. Instrument find out the challenging applications for pH, ion concentration, conductivity, dissolved oxygen.

**BOD Respirometric sensor system**

**Make**: Velp

**Model**: F10200270

**Equipment Description**: BOD respirometric sensor system is easy to setup and allows for quick sample preparation for BOD analysis. Display that continuously update results. View the results at any time during the result. The instrument plot a curve of BOD over time.

**COD Digester**

**Make**: Hach

**Model**: DRB200

**Equipment Description**: Use the COD digester for the digestion of samples control temperature in the reactor from 20 to 150˚c in less than 10 minutes.

**Water quality field kit**

**Make**: Systronics

**Model**: Systronics /371

**Equipment Description**: This instrument measures pH, mv/ORP, conductivity/TDS, salinity, D.O, temperature, colorimeter, Turbidity.

**Jar test Apparatus**

**Make**: GenNext

**Model**: cx-406

**Equipment Description**: Jar test is used to find the optimum dose of coagulant (Alum).

**Membrane Filtration unit**

**Make**: GenNext

**Model**: SDF1-15

**Equipment Description**: Membrane filtration is a pressure driven separation process that employs a membrane for both mechanical and chemical sieving of particles and macromolecules.

**Orbital Shaker**

**Make**: Remi

**Model**: Remi/CIS-18

**Equipment Description**: Orbital shaking incubator finds application in researches on bacteria, cultivation, fermentation, hybridization, chemical and bio chemical reactions, enzymes and tissue research, which have a high requirement on precision of shaking speed and temp.

**Hot Air Oven**

**Make**: MAC

**Model**: MAC-HAOD-125L-pro

**Equipment Description**: Hot Air oven use extremely high temperatures over several hours to destroy microorganisms and bacterial spores. Temperature range (50˚ to 250˚c).

**Magnetic stirrer**

**Make**: IKA

**Model**: RCT B S022

**Equipment Description**: Magnetic stirrer uses a rotating magnetic field to move a stir bar around in liquid samples. Hot plate magnetic stirrer keeps liquid circulating as they are heated for faster reaction.

**Analytical balance**

**Make**: Shimadzu

**Model**: Shimadzu-AP 225wd

**Equipment Description**: A weighing balance is an instrument which is used to determine the weight or mass of an object. Weighs in the range of 0.1g to 220 g.

**Vortex Mixer**

**Make**: IKA

**Model**: VORTEX S022

**Equipment Description**: Vortex mixers are one of the primary technologies for mixing laboratories in tubes, well plates or flasks

**Muffle furnace**

**Make**: MAC

**Model**: MSW 25

**Equipment Description**: Muffle furnace are used for high temperature testing application such as loss-on ignition or ashing.

**Biosafety cabinet**

**Make**: Thermo- scientific

**Model**: 1300 Series A2

**Equipment Description**: It is an enclosed, ventilated laboratory workspace for safely working with materials contaminated with pathogens for requiring a defined biosafety level.

**Steam Sterilizer Autoclave**

**Make**: MAC

**Model**: AV-22 (eco)

**Equipment Description**: Autoclave is a machine that uses steam under pressure to kill harmful bacteria, viruses, fungi on items that are placed inside a pressure vessels.

**Non-refrigerated centrifuge**

**Make**: Remi

**Model**: Remi/ RM-03 Plus

**Equipment Description**: A centrifuge used to separate materials from small samples. It is used for spinning a variety of samples. Max speed upto13,300 rpm

**Solid phase extractor manifold system**

**Make**: Cole parmer

**Model**: 07532-45/NC30746

**Equipment Description**: SPE is a technique designed for rapid, selective sample preparation and purification prior to the chromatography analysis.

**Colorimeter**

**Make**: Systronics

**Model**: Systronics/115

**Equipment Description**: Colorimeter measures percentage Transmittance and Absorbance. A wide range of wavelength of 400 to 700 nm is covered with the help of 8 optical filters mounted on a turret.

**BOD incubator**

**Make**: Remi

**Model**: Remi/ RM-03 Plus

**Equipment Description**: Cooling incubator are designed to maintain a constant temp.(5˚ to 60˚c).These chambers are used for BOD test culture of bacteria, microorganism, tissue culture, incubation etc.

**Double distillation unit**

**Make**: JSGW

**Model**: JSGW/427/2

**Equipment Description**: Double distillation unit that disinfect water, reduce the concentration of toxic metals and remove some organic contaminants.

**Fume hood**

**Make**: citizen

**Model**: AFA-1000

**Equipment Description**: A fume hood is a ventilated, enclosed work space intended to capture, contain, and exhaust harmful or dangerous fumes, vapours, and particulate matter generated by procedures conducted within the fume hood.

**Water bath**

**Make**: Thermo scientific

**Model**: TSSWB15

**Equipment Description**: Water bath is a equipment that is used to incubate samples at constant temperature over a long period of time.

**Flame Photometer**

**Make**: LABTRONICS

**Model**: LT-671

**Equipment Description**: Flame photometer is an ideal instrument for determination of concentrations of sodium, potassium, calcium, lithium & barium in the samples. It is based on microcontroller technology which gives accurate and reliable results.

**Kjeltron Nitrogen Estimation System**

**Make**: Tulin

**Model**: KDIGB 6M,KjelSCRB,KjelDIST EA

**Equipment Description**: Total Kjeldahl Nitrogen (TKN) analysis determines both the organic and the inorganic forms of nitrogen.

**Microscope**

**Make**: micron OPTIK

**Model**: CRESTA zs-50i FL

**Equipment Description**: A fluorescent microscope is a type of an Optical microscope which uses fluorescence technology to generate precise images of organic and inorganic substances. The basic principle behind the working of such microscopes is the emission of light within specific wavelengths which produce high power LED images.

**Sonicator**

**Make**: LABWAN

**Model**: lw-ss-7

**Equipment Description**: ultrasonic cleaning is based on the cavitation effect caused by high frequency ultrasonic wave vibration signal in the fluid. Microscopic bubbles are formed and they burst violently causing the cavitation which creates an intense scrubbing action on the surface of the item being cleaned. The bubbles are small enough to penetrate microscopic crevices, cleaning them thoroughly and consistently.

**Respirable dust sampler**

**Make**: Envirotech

**Model**: APM460 NL

**Equipment Description**: APM 460 sampler uses an improved cyclone (with sharper cutoff- D 50 at 10 microns) to separate the coarser particles (>10 microns) from the air stream before filtering it on the glass microfiber filter. Using APM 460 measurement of Respirable particulate matter is possible with an accuracy of ±5% TSPM can also be assessed by collection of dust retained in the cyclone cup. Specifications: Flow rate- 0.9-1.4 m3/min free flow. Particle size- A cyclone is used for fractioning the dust into two fractions. D -50 for the cyclone is at 10 microns. PM 10 dust is accumulated on the filter paper. While coarse dust is collected in a cup placed under the cyclone. Sampling time-28 hours (maximum). Automatic sampling control-24 hrs. Programmable digital timer is used to shut off the sampler automatically after a preset time interval.

**Fine particulate sampler**

**Make**: Envirotech

**Model**: APM 550MFC

**Equipment Description**: APM 550 system has been designed for regular monitoring PM 2.5 in ambient air. Also, since it allows the removal of PM 2.5 from the sample stream so the same system may be optionally used as a PM10 Sampler. APM 550 can be easily paired with a gaseous sampling attachment (for monitoring SO2, NOx, NH3, Ozone etc.) as gaseous sampling requires only a few LPM of air flow.

The Structural Engineering division is focused on creation of new materials tailored for different uses, conventional and new methods for infrastructure design, innovative construction techniques, developing techniques for health monitoring of existing and new structures, developing methods for strengthening aged infrastructure, and smart infrastructure for natural and built environment.

The infrastructure part includes buildings, tunnels, bridges, and other built environment structures. Structural Engineering division assists with all phases of building infrastructure, from conceptualization to complete design and construction, as well as assessment and strengthening. Both traditional and new structural materials are the part of ongoing business.

Our researchers deal with advanced and innovative analysis methods, which together with experiments, lead to deeper understanding and better knowledge of the behaviour of different components, connections and the structure as a whole. Considering the terrain of Jammu & Kashmir, Structural Engineering division also closely works on the structural part of the Tunnel systems and collaboratively deals with ongoing tunnelling projects at different parts of J&K region. This makes it possible to develop safer and more effective structures to meet the future demands for sustainable development, human security and public health and above all, nation building.

Research areas:

1. Behaviour of quasi-brittle materials under non-uniform passive and active confinement.
2. RC structures under monotonic and cyclic loading.
3. Prestressed concrete
4. Fatigue and Fracture
5. Concrete wind turbine towers
6. Damage assessment in existing structures
7. Durability of RC structures
8. Concrete Damage Plasticity
9. Fatigue behaviour of polymer composites
10. Fluid flow modelling in fracturing porous materials
11. Fracture behaviour of polymer composites
12. FRP strengthening
13. Homogenization of composite materials and multiscale modelling
14. Impact testing
15. Application of Machine Learning
16. Meta structures
17. Microstructure analysis
18. Multi-scale multi-phase modelling of Geomaterial
19. Non-destructive Evaluation of Concrete Structures using Ultrasound
20. Numerical modelling of elastic wave propagation and scattering
21. Polymer composites
22. Seismic analysis of RCC structures
23. Spectral element method (SEM)
24. Subsurface defect detection and characterization
25. Subsurface imaging
26. Subsurface imaging and characterization
27. Surrogate modelling
28. Ultra high performance concrete (UHPC)
29. Ultrasonic Non-destructive evaluation

Transportation engineering deals with the planning, functional design, operation and management of facilities for any mode of transportation in order to provide safe, efficient, and environmentally compatible movement of people and goods. The Transportation Engineering Division at IIT Jammu is one of the fastest growing departments in the country working with an aim to provide sustainable and safe solutions to transportation problems.

The Transportation Engineering Division laboratory is well equipped with advanced research facilities like Corelok Systems, automatic asphalt content tester, V-Box (Data Logger), and advanced two-wheeler driving simulator. The division is currently working in the major research areas of driving behaviour analysis, surrogate safety measures, public transport systems, traffic control and management, and pavement materials. Further, considering the shortage of good quality aggregates in the Jammu & Kashmir region, the division research group is exploring the various material treatment methods for utilizing inferior aggregates as a pavement construction material.

Research Areas:

1. Characterization of pavement materials.
2. Design and performance evaluation of flexible pavements.
3. Driver behaviour profiling.
4. Driving behaviour analysis.
5. Exclusive two-wheeler lane.
6. Hot, warm and cold mix asphalt.
7. Intelligent transportation systems.
8. Passenger Satisfaction.
9. Public transport planning and design.
10. Road safety – Surrogate safety Measures.
11. Traffic flow theory and capacity analysis..
12. Traffic management, operations and control.

- #### Water Resource Engineering

- Water Resource Engineering
- Ph.D Students
- Facility

The Water Resources Engineering program at Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu, aims to provide support to undergraduate and PhD students to have the opportunity to understand and practically visualize the fluid mechanics and open channel hydraulics principles. Water Resources laboratory has hydrology equipments like rainfall-runoff simulator, rain gauge, pan evaporimeter, infiltrometers and many fluid mechanics apparatus to understand the basic principles of flow behaviour in pipe flow and open channel flows.  In this lab, research is carried in the diversified areas such as climate change, hydrological and hydraulic modelling, river engineering works and satellite remote sensing for water resources applications. In this specialization, the under graduate students have opportunity to study core and elective courses on fluid mechanics and open channel hydraulics, engineering hydrology, river engineering, design of hydraulic structures and water resources management.

Research Areas:

- Hydrological and Hydraulic Modelling
- Contaminant Transport and Ground Water Hydrology
- Climate Change
- Crop Modelling
- River Dynamics and River Engineering
- Experimental Hydraulics
- Satellite Remote Sensing
